_meta.purpose: Reconcile the two source taxonomies into one canonical disease-area enum with an explicit mapping table, per Day 1 of the brief.
_meta.canonical_choice: The canonical set is BIO/QLS (2021)'s 14 major disease areas plus 'Others' (15 values). BIO/QLS is chosen as canonical because it is the finer-grained taxonomy and is the engine's primary baseline source. Wong (2019)'s 9 therapeutic groups are coarser and map one-to-(one-or-many) onto the canonical set.
_meta.unmapped_note: Wong has no separate group for Hematology, Allergy, Gastroenterology, or Respiratory (they are absorbed into Wong's 'Overall'/unreported buckets), so those canonical areas map to null on the Wong side. 'Others' likewise has no Wong equivalent.
_meta.vaccines_note: Wong's 'Vaccines (Infectious Disease)' is a therapeutic group in Wong but is really a modality subset within infectious disease; at the disease-area level it maps to 'Infectious disease'. Vaccine-as-modality lives in the modality/novelty tables, not here.
